_comment: Audit routing configuration for AIRP dependency governance. Edit this file to change which licenses auto-pass, which require audit, and which are blocked. See README.md for the policy anchors (DEV-GUIDE.md §7.1, AGENTS.md third-party rules).

inventory_routing._comment: Classifies the CURRENT dependency inventory (used by SBOM/notices generation, #190). Driven by license + scope, not version bumps.

auto_pass._comment: Licenses that are permissive and compatible with AIRP's MIT OR Apache-2.0 distribution. Still recorded in the SBOM but do not trigger a dedicated audit. Composite expressions (OR/AND/WITH) are classified leaf-by-leaf by the AST parser in audit-routing.mjs, so only single SPDX ids need to be listed here.

audit_required._comment: Licenses that need a dedicated audit before release. Weak copyleft (file-level isolation), copyleft in dev-only scope, non-commercial, or unknown licenses.

block._comment: Licenses that block release. Strong copyleft in runtime/link dependencies would force the whole AIRP binary to copyleft, incompatible with MIT OR Apache-2.0 distribution. Also: unlicensed code, proprietary, no-license.

upgrade_routing._comment: Classifies proposed version upgrades (DEV-GUIDE.md §7.1). Determines whether an upgrade can be an auto-PR or must route to a deduplicated GitHub issue with a dedicated audit.

patch._comment: Same major.minor, patch bump (e.g. 1.2.3 -> 1.2.4). Auto-PR allowed; PR must carry version diff, upstream release/security links, audit result, and gating by impact scope. Auto-PR still subject to audit bot + human review.

minor._comment: Major >= 1, minor bump (e.g. 1.2.x -> 1.3.0). Issue required; audit changelog, migration/deprecation, Node/Rust/platform floors, plugin/runtime compat, license/provenance, security advisories, data/build artifact changes, rollback path.

major._comment: Major bump (e.g. 1.x -> 2.0.0). Issue required; same audit scope as minor.

0x-minor._comment: 0.x dependency minor bump (e.g. 0.1.2 -> 0.1.3). Treated as major risk per DEV-GUIDE §7.1. Issue required.

prerelease._comment: Target is a prerelease (alpha/beta/rc/dev). Never auto-adopted. Issue required even if the stable major would otherwise auto-pass.

patch-sensitive._comment: Patch bump that touches data format, network/permission boundary, cryptography, or build/release chain. Escalated from auto-pr to issue per DEV-GUIDE §7.1. Override of patch.

sensitive_areas._comment: Substring matches against the dependency name or its configured area tags. A patch bump matching any of these is reclassified patch -> patch-sensitive (issue required).

dedup._comment: Issue dedup key. Two upgrade proposals with the same key MUST update the same issue, never create a second one. Key = ecosystem:name:target-version. Class is NOT part of the key (a re-classification updates the existing issue).

first_party._comment: Workspace members and AIRP first-party crates. These are not third-party dependencies; they auto-pass inventory routing and are excluded from third-party notices (they carry AIRP's own MIT OR Apache-2.0 license).
